findings.problem: The chapter's Saturday-morning sequence conflicts with the provided canon in two linked ways: line 97 makes Paul the police caller even though the boundary excerpt already established Tommy as the one talking to the dispatcher, and lines 97-149 compress Mallick's arrival to less than an hour after the deputies reach the house even though the continuity sheet preserves a deputy-only window before her later entrance. As written, the reader has to invent an unseen extra call and a missing stretch of time to reconcile the chapter with the allowed context packs.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:97, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:107, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:145, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:149 so the morning chronology matches canon. Either make Paul's study call an explicit follow-up that does not replace Tommy's already-established dispatcher call, or remove/reframe the exact timing language so Mallick's entrance no longer reads as happening almost immediately after the 8:30 deputy beat.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can read the revised morning movement anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:97, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:107, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:149 without having to invent an off-page extra call or a missing ninety-minute gap: Tommy is still legible as the initial dispatcher caller, and Mallick's arrival no longer reads as occurring within roughly thirty to forty minutes of the deputies' arrival. Fail if Paul still appears to make the primary police call or if the detective still seems to arrive almost immediately after the deputies.

findings.problem: Mallick's entrance dialogue echoes the style-bible calibration lines too closely. The wrong-name apology, the casserole explanation tied to her mother's empty-handed rule, and the rambling powder-room request arrive with nearly the same phrasing and sequencing as the non-copyable examples, so the scene reads less like live speech than like voice notes pasted into the manuscript.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite Detective Mallick's entrance at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:149, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:155, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:159-167 to preserve the required factual beats (wrong name, casserole, bathroom request) while giving her new sentence shapes, new tangents, and a fresh self-correction pattern specific to this moment.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:149, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:155, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:159 still contains the three required beats but no longer hinges on the current 'my mother always said you don't show up empty-handed' formulation or the current Kessner-to-Kessler apology cadence. Fail if Mallick still sounds like a lightly paraphrased style-bible demo rather than a fresh scene-specific speaker.

findings.problem: Tommy's POV keeps translating pressure through the same coaching-game scaffold until it starts to feel automatic rather than load-bearing. One or two sports conversions would be character-true; six major ones in a chapter this short flatten the panic into a repeated template and crowd out fresher textures of bodily shame, house geography, and evidence fear.
findings.rewrite_direction: Trim or replace several of the sports-metaphor clusters at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:23, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:35, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:39, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:81, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:101, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:171-175. Keep the failed halftime-talk beat and at most one later callback; render the remaining pressure beats through concrete bodily or situational detail already present in the scene.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if at least three of the cited beats in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:23, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:35, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:39, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:81, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:101, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:171 are rewritten without football, game, season, sideline, or timeout framing, while Tommy's spoken cadence still reads like a coach. Fail if most of those moments still explain the same panic via repeated sports analogies.

findings.problem: Several pressure beats slide out of Tommy's blunt close-third into polished abstract capstones that sound more admired than lived: the heart 'felt structural rather than rhythmic,' the bathroom light makes the room seem 'remembered rather than seen,' the bathroom trap becomes 'the specific helplessness' of a role, and the final light is 'very bright and very specific' as Tommy 'received it.' Those lines pull the chapter toward prestige-literary solemnity exactly where the style bible asks for muscular dark-comic immediacy.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the interpretive capstones at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:83, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:135, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:171-175, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:189 so the scene stays in Tommy's concrete sensory and bodily register. Keep the same dramatic beats, but let the image or action carry the pressure instead of abstractly naming what the reader should feel.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised versions of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:83, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:135, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:171-175, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:189 communicate Tommy's panic through concrete physical or environmental facts rather than essay-like summary phrases. Fail if those beats still end in abstract interpretive language that asks the reader to admire the sentence instead of track the immediate scene.

summary: The chapter has a strong panic engine and several vivid dark-comic images, but it does not pass review. A canon-breaking morning chronology, a high-severity calibration echo in Mallick's entrance, and prose that leans too hard on repeated sports scaffolds and polished abstract capstones keep the chapter below pass-level control.
